test.name: Protestant German states Julian-to-Gregorian 1700 (10-day shift)
test.description: Convert a Protestant German date from 1699 — before the Protestant German states adopted the Gregorian calendar in February/March 1700. The shift for this jurisdiction-and-era is 10 days. Tests the middle band of Gregorian adoption — between Catholic Europe (1582) and Britain (1752).

input.user_message: Convert this Württemberg birth record date to the modern Gregorian calendar: '15 January 1699'.

judge_context: Should recognize that Protestant German states like Württemberg adopted the Gregorian calendar in February/March 1700, so a January 1699 date is still Julian
Should apply the correct 10-day offset for this jurisdiction-and-era — NOT 11 days (England 1752) or 13 days (Russia 1918)
Should produce '25 January 1699' as the Gregorian equivalent
Should preserve the original Julian date in the output, presenting both forms
Should distinguish Protestant German states (1700 adoption) from Catholic German states (1583-1585 adoption) — they're 100+ years apart
